# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_zapit (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `zapi` (String) A dictionary for the zapi and arguments. An XML tag *<tag>value</tag>* is a dictionary with tag as the key. Value can be another dictionary, a list of dictionaries, a string, or nothing. eg *<tag/>* is represented as *tag:* A single zapi can be called at a time.  Ansible warns if duplicate keys are found and only uses the last entry.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vserver` (String) if provided, forces vserver tunneling.  username identifies a cluster admin account.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `errno` (String) If the ZAPI was executed but failed, the error code set by the ZAPI. Not present if successful, or if the ZAPI call cannot be performed.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `reason` (String) If the ZAPI was executed but failed, the error reason set by the ZAPI. Not present if successful, or if the ZAPI call cannot be performed.
- `response` (String) If successful, a json dictionary representing the data returned by the ZAPI. If the ZAPI was executed but failed, an empty dictionary. Not present if the ZAPI call cannot be performed.
- `status` (String) If the ZAPI was executed but failed, the status set by the ZAPI. Not present if successful, or if the ZAPI call cannot be performed.
